condition.selected: I picked the line closest to the top of the bar

incorrects.description: Student selected closest line strategy, which doesn't use the halfway pattern

steps.text: The closest line is 50, but the bar shows 45. That strategy gives you an estimate, not the exact value. What pattern did we discover about halfway between the lines?

condition.selected: I added the two numbers on the lines

incorrects.description: Student selected adding strategy, which is incorrect for finding halfway

steps.text: Adding 40 and 50 gives 90, not 45. Think about the pattern we found. When the scale is 10, how much more is halfway between any two lines?

incorrects.description: Student selected guessing, missing the halfway pattern

steps.text: You don't need to guess. We learned a pattern. When the scale is 10, halfway between any two lines is always 5 more than the lower line. That's the strategy.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Ghost gridline at 45 pulses. Visual shows 40 to halfway equals 5 more.

steps.text: Here's the pattern. The scale is 10, so each big jump is 10. Half of 10 is 5. That means halfway between any two lines is 5 more than the lower line. The bar is at 45, halfway between 40 and 50. The answer is: I found halfway. That's always 5 more.
